name: supabase
description: Navigate Supabase database tables, relationships, and query patterns. Use when you need to understand how tables connect, write queries, or find the right data source.
capabilities: Browse 165 database objects including tables, views, and partitions
View table relationships with foreign key constraints documented
Find TypeScript type definitions for 35 database tables
Access common query patterns and join examples
Locate database functions and RPC calls
author: Acurioustractor
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown files. No executable code, scripts, network calls, file system access, or environment variable reads. The skill is a knowledge base for database schema reference.
valueStatement: Supabase database navigation is complex. This skill provides instant access to table relationships, query patterns, and type definitions for your project. Save time searching through migrations and documentation.
limitations: Does not execute queries or modify database state
Does not provide real-time schema validation
Cannot connect to external Supabase projects
Documentation is specific to this project schema

useCases.title: Build Data Features
useCases.description: Understand table relationships to implement new features that touch the database

useCases.title: Write Database Queries
useCases.description: Find the correct table structure and foreign keys for writing Supabase queries

useCases.title: Explore Data Model
useCases.description: Navigate the database schema to understand how data flows through the system

promptTemplates.title: List Tables
promptTemplates.prompt: What tables exist for user management in the Supabase database?

promptTemplates.title: Find Relationship
promptTemplates.prompt: How do stories and organizations connect? Show me the foreign key relationship.

promptTemplates.title: Query Example
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a Supabase query to get stories with their storyteller profile using proper joins.

promptTemplates.title: Type Location
promptTemplates.prompt: Where are the TypeScript types for the stories table and what fields do they include?

bestPractices: Always filter queries by tenant_id for multi-tenant data isolation
Use proper foreign key joins instead of multiple separate queries
Reference the correct TypeScript type file for your domain
antiPatterns: Querying tables without tenant_id filter breaks data isolation
Using UK spelling (organisations) in Supabase queries (requires US spelling)
Hardcoding values instead of using database functions for complex queries

faq.question: Which Supabase client should I use?
faq.answer: Browser client for React components, Server SSR for API routes, Service Role for admin operations only.

faq.question: How many tables are in the database?
faq.answer: 165 objects including 153 tables, 7 views, and 3 partitions with 35 tables having TypeScript types.

faq.question: Can this skill execute SQL queries?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides documentation and patterns only. Use Supabase MCP tools or API routes to execute queries.

faq.question: Is my data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only reads documentation files. It cannot access, modify, or query any database.

faq.question: Why do some tables show ORPHANED?
faq.answer: Tables without migration files or TypeScript types. They exist in the live database but lack source-controlled definitions.

faq.question: How is this different from Supabase MCP?
faq.answer: This skill provides project-specific documentation. Supabase MCP provides direct database access and query execution.
